prevalence.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease Study 2019 (projected)
prevalence.trend: Stable to slightly increasing due to aging population

incidence.source: Indian Journal of Rheumatology and GBD estimates

vulnerablePopulations: Women (due to hormonal and load-bearing factors)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis based on history and physical examination (e.g., pain lasting >3 months for chronic LBP); Red flags for serious pathology (e.g., cauda equina syndrome, infection, fracture); Imaging (X-ray, MRI) only if red flags present or non-responsive to conservative treatment; Guidelines from American College of Physicians adapted locally

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: No routine screening; opportunistic in occupational health (~10-20% coverage in formal sectors)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low (~30-40% effective management; many rely on self-medication)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Similar to international standards; emphasis on clinical assessment due to limited imaging access; Integration of Ayurvedic diagnostics in some areas
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Conservative: NSAIDs, physiotherapy, exercise (e.g., McKenzie method); Multidisciplinary approach per Indian Orthopaedic Association guidelines; Yoga and alternative therapies encouraged; Surgery for <5% (e.g., disc herniation); Opioids restricted
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access in rural areas (70% population); Over-reliance on informal providers and quackery; High out-of-pocket costs (~70% healthcare expenditure); Diagnostic delays due to resource constraints
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Integration of traditional practices like yoga and Ayurveda; Growing public awareness campaigns; Affordable generic medications; Community-based rehabilitation programs in urban centers
